steps.title: Find the GCF of both terms
steps.content:
Examine $64t^2s^3$ and $56t^3s$:
  • Coefficients: GCF(64, 56) = 4 (since $64 = 4 \cdot 16$ and $56 = 4 \cdot 14$)
  • Powers of $t$: minimum power is $t^2$
  • Powers of $s$: minimum power is $s$
Overall GCF = $4t^2s$

steps.title: Divide each term by the GCF
steps.content:
Divide both terms by $4t^2s$:
  • $\frac{64t^2s^3}{4t^2s} = 16s^2$
  • $\frac{56t^3s}{4t^2s} = 14t$
Therefore, $64t^2s^3 - 56t^3s = 4t^2s(16s^2 - 14t)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
